blockers.description: Phase 43 verification returned human_needed — 2 items require manual testing with real Pushover credentials
blockers.workaround: Automated tests pass (6/6). Human UAT items are: (1) Run app with real instance and verify Pushover notification arrives, (2) Stop instance during 30s monitor timeout and confirm no spurious notification

human_actions_pending.action: Run full application with real instance and verify Pushover notification delivery
human_actions_pending.context: Verification requires real nanobot process logging 'Starting Telegram bot' and real Pushover credentials

human_actions_pending.action: Stop instance during 30s monitor timeout window and confirm no spurious notifications
human_actions_pending.context: Verifies stop-before-monitor-completes cancellation works correctly with real processes

decisions.decision: Notifier injected via constructor chain (main→manager→lifecycle) using duck-typing interface
decisions.rationale: Avoids circular imports between instance and notifier packages

decisions.decision: TelegramMonitor lifecycle tied to InstanceLifecycle (start after process start, cancel before process stop)
decisions.rationale: Ensures monitor only runs when instance is alive; clean shutdown prevents goroutine leaks

decisions.decision: Verification status set to human_needed rather than passed
decisions.rationale: Automated tests verify wiring with mocks, but end-to-end delivery path requires real Pushover credentials

next_action: Run /gsd-verify-work to perform human UAT, or approve verification checkpoint to complete phase and update roadmap
context_notes: Phase 43 execution complete. Both plans executed successfully (43-01: wiring, 43-02: TDD tests). All 6 monitor tests pass. Build passes. Verification found no code issues — only needs real Pushover credential testing. Phase 42 VERIFICATION.md also contains TBD placeholders. The v0.9 milestone has phases 41-43, phases 41 and 42 are already complete. After phase 43 human verification, v0.9 milestone may be ready for completion.
